quick_facts.label: Recruitment category
quick_facts.value: Senior Clerk cum Typist was notified as a graduate-level Non-Technical Popular Category (NTPC) post through Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) in CEN 05/2024.

quick_facts.label: Education requirement
quick_facts.value: The CEN 05/2024 post-parameter table required a degree from a recognised university or equivalent, with typing proficiency in English or Hindi on computer described as essential.

quick_facts.label: Typing requirement
quick_facts.value: For CEN 05/2024, the qualifying Typing Skill Test required 30 words per minute in English or 25 words per minute in Hindi on a personal computer, without editing tools or spell-check; its marks were not added to merit.

quick_facts.label: Pay level
quick_facts.value: In CEN 05/2024, the post was in 7th CPC Pay Level 5 with stated initial basic pay of ₹29,200. This is dated recruitment-notice context, not a current take-home-pay estimate.

quick_facts.label: Medical standard
quick_facts.value: The CEN 05/2024 post-parameter table specified medical standard C-2 for Senior Clerk cum Typist; appointment remained subject to the Railway Administration’s medical-fitness examination and verification.

sections.heading: Entry through RRB NTPC (graduate posts)
paragraphs: The principal open-market route evidenced by the latest detailed graduate NTPC notice is an online application to one chosen RRB for the notified Senior Clerk cum Typist vacancies. In CEN 05/2024, applicants selected post and Railway/Unit preferences in the application; final allotment was subject to merit, medical standard and vacancies. That notice’s application dates and age limits were specific to the 2024 recruitment cycle, so future aspirants should rely on the next applicable official CEN rather than treat those dates as continuing rules.

The CEN 05/2024 selection sequence was: first-stage CBT, second-stage CBT, a qualifying Typing Skill Test for this post, document verification and medical examination. The RRB uses second-stage CBT performance to shortlist candidates for the typing test; final merit for candidates who qualify the test, or receive a permitted PwBD exemption, is based on second-stage CBT performance. RRBs recommend empanelled candidates, while the concerned Railway Administration issues the appointment offer after the remaining conditions are satisfied.
bullets: CEN 05/2024’s graduate-post eligibility was a university degree or equivalent; candidates awaiting final results were instructed not to apply.

CEN 05/2024 temporarily used an 18–36 age band as of January 1, 2025, including a one-time three-year COVID-19-related relaxation. Age rules and relaxations must be checked afresh in each new notification.
The typing test was qualifying rather than merit-scoring: 30 WPM in English or 25 WPM in Hindi on computer, without spell-check or editing tools.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: Computer typing accuracy and speed in either English or Hindi are directly assessed for this role. The official notice also makes clear that the test is performed on a personal computer without editing or spell-check assistance, so candidates should practise clean first-pass typing rather than relying on correction tools.

For the written stages, the published NTPC graduate syllabus covered mathematics, general intelligence and reasoning, and general awareness; it expressly included basics of computers and computer applications within general awareness. These are examination-preparation requirements, while the role’s essential stated skill is computer typing proficiency.
bullets: English typing: target at least 30 WPM for the qualifying benchmark in CEN 05/2024.
Hindi typing: target at least 25 WPM for the qualifying benchmark in CEN 05/2024; the notice provided Kruti Dev and Mangal fonts for the Hindi test.
Develop practical computer literacy alongside typing, including reliable keyboard use and familiarity with routine computer applications.

sections.heading: Railway office placement and mobility
paragraphs: The 2024 graduate NTPC notice placed Senior Clerk cum Typist vacancies across Zonal Railways and Production Units of Indian Railways. Thus, the employing context is a railway administrative establishment rather than a single nationally fixed office or city; the available units and vacancy counts vary by notification.

The notice stated that, ordinarily, a railway servant is employed throughout service in the Railway or establishment of first appointment and has no right to a transfer to another Railway or establishment. It also warned newly appointed candidates that transfer requests to another Railway would generally not be registered until at least five to ten years of service had been completed. This is important when selecting RRB, post and Railway/Unit preferences.
bullets: Candidates were asked to submit a single application to one RRB and state preferences for eligible posts and Railway/Unit options.
Railway Administration may transfer an employee in service exigencies, including to another department, establishment or project.

sections.heading: Ministerial-cadre context
paragraphs: An Indian Railways HRMS cadre-management document illustrates the ministerial staff hierarchy as Junior Clerk (Pay Level 2), Senior Clerk (Pay Level 5), Office Superintendent (Pay Level 6), and Chief Office Superintendent (Pay Level 7). It therefore provides useful context for the Senior Clerk cum Typist post as a Level-5 clerical position within a broader ministerial cadre.

This hierarchy should not be read as an automatic promotion timetable. Vacancies, seniority arrangements, applicable Railway Board rules, departmental selection processes and the employee’s cadre or unit determine actual advancement. The RRB NTPC notice itself only governs direct recruitment and appointment for its notified vacancies.
bullets: Illustrative hierarchy: Junior Clerk → Senior Clerk → Office Superintendent → Chief Office Superintendent.
The cited HRMS material gives the Senior Clerk level as Level 5, Office Superintendent as Level 6, and Chief Office Superintendent as Level 7.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: The most recent detailed graduate NTPC notice located for this role, CEN 05/2024, listed Senior Clerk cum Typist in 7th CPC Pay Level 5 with initial basic pay of ₹29,200. This figure is a dated, notification-specific basic-pay reference, not a statement of net monthly salary.

Actual monthly earnings can differ from the listed basic pay because payroll outcomes depend on then-applicable allowances, deductions, posting-related circumstances and government revisions. Applicants should treat the pay level and basic-pay figure in the live official notification as the authoritative reference for a future recruitment cycle.
bullets: CEN 05/2024: Pay Level 5; initial basic pay ₹29,200.

CEN 05/2024: medical standard C-2 and 732 notified Senior Clerk cum Typist vacancies across all RRBs before subsequent changes, illustrating why vacancy totals should always be checked against notices and corrigenda.
